summary: Armada ensemble: Twilio SMS + email marketing backend for adriancortez worker
logs: [gen:sms-twilio:opus] failed: API Error: Connection closed mid-response. The response above may be incomplete.

output.rationale:
I read the actual repo (migrations/0002_crm.sql, src/crm.js, src/worker.js, wrangler.toml, docs/admin/index.html) before merging, which let me settle the disputes between the two stitches on evidence rather than prose.

WHICH STITCH IS THE STRONGER BASE: Stitch B, because its architecture is both more correct and more complete against the verified codebase:
1. Suppression as a SQL NOT EXISTS anti-join (one subrequest) vs. Stitch A's per-contact isSuppressed() loop inside resolveRecipients (N subrequests). On a real list of hundreds of contacts, A's loop is a genuine Workers subrequest-cap risk. B wins decisively; I kept B's anti-join and demoted isSuppressed() to only the single-row at-send-time recheck.
2. B ships the Twilio inbound webhook with the CORRECT signature scheme (HMAC-SHA1 + base64 of the raw digest, per Twilio's actual spec) and the /u/:token unsubscribe page. A deferred both to sibling sections. For a "data-and-api" backend these compliance endpoints are load-bearing, and B's crypto is right where a SHA-256 attempt would reject every real webhook.
3. B re-queues transient 429/5xx instead of marking them permanently failed (A even lists the missing retry as an open question).
4. B's "marketing reads contacts, CRM owns create/edit" split turned out to exactly match reality: the admin panel ALREADY has a CRM tab (real tabs array line 164 is ["leads","crm","listings","copy"]), and crm.js already has full contact CRUD. A duplicate marketing contact-CRUD (which A implied) would fight the existing CRM.

WHAT I GRAFTED FROM STITCH A: A returns recipient_count on campaign creation for an immediate composer preview; I added that to B's POST /campaigns (B only returned {id,status}).

WHAT I FIXED IN BOTH (neither got these, because neither actually read docs/admin/index.html or noticed handleCrm's return contract):
- BOTH claimed the tabs array was ["leads","listings","copy"]. The real array is ["leads","crm","listings","copy"] — a CRM tab already exists. Corrected the UI-contract note.
- handleCrm() returns a Response for EVERY path (its own 404 at line 167), so worker.js line 108 is an unconditional `return handleCrm(...)`. handleAdminMarketing() returns null on miss, so it must be dispatched with `if (res) return res;`. I made this asymmetry explicit so the human wires it correctly and the admin-block 404 still fires for real misses.
- There is currently NO [[d1_databases]] block in wrangler.toml (crm.js expects env.DB but the binding was never added), so this section must add it. Both stitches hedged "if CRM already added it" but the real file shows it must be added here.

Everything else preserved from the shared-good set both attempts agreed on: resolveRecipients single source of truth, {confirm:"SEND", expected_count} 409-on-drift, at-send-time consent/suppression recheck, chunked cron (scheduled->sending->drain->finalize), idempotent enqueue via UNIQUE(campaign_id,contact_id)+INSERT OR IGNORE, DST-correct quiet hours via Intl, idempotent STOP-notice append, expiring signed unsub token with List-Unsubscribe one-click headers, live GROUP BY stats, no auto-re-consent on suppression removal, and reuse of normPhone() from crm.js.
open_questions: The [[d1_databases]] DB binding is shared with crm.js and must appear exactly once in wrangler.toml. The real file currently has none, so this section adds it; if the CRM/other section also emits one at stitch time, keep a single block.

The Twilio inbound webhook (/api/hooks/twilio) and /u/:token unsubscribe page are included here because a data/API backend owns compliance. If a separate sms-twilio/email section also ships them, dedupe at stitch time — this file is self-contained and can own them, or its sendSmsMessage/sendEmailMessage seams can be swapped for the sibling section's (they are the only coupling point).
PUBLIC_BASE_URL defaults to https://adriancortez.com; confirm the production apex before the first real email send so unsubscribe links resolve.

The admin UI section must extend the real tabs array (line 164, currently ["leads","crm","listings","copy"]) to add a "campaigns" tab and matching section, and decide whether the marketing Contacts view is its own tab or folds into the existing CRM tab. Contact create/edit stays in CRM; the marketing side is read + the narrow consent/tags PATCH only.

output.rationale:
Both Stitch A and Stitch B never inspected the repo and shared one fatal blindspot: they invented a `contacts` table and a `0001_marketing.sql` that COLLIDE with the CRM already shipped at commit 709366a (migrations/0002_crm.sql created contacts). I read src/worker.js, src/crm.js, docs/admin/{index.html,crm.js}, migrations/0002_crm.sql, wrangler.toml, and armada/BRIEF.md to ground the merge. The brief itself was stale (it references public/admin/ which no longer exists and predates the CRM).

DECISIONS on the correct base + grafts:
- CORRECTNESS BASE = Stitch A's engine (byte-verified Twilio HMAC-SHA1+base64; I re-proved parity with twilio-node's algorithm: FuPWlV0BuWvojWa7d0vDtxEOz0E= == FuPWlV0BuWvojWa7d0vDtxEOz0E=). Both stitches had this right; I kept A's batched enqueue that inserts suppressed rows for complete campaign stats (verified: 1 queued / 3 suppressed for a 4-contact segment). B's set-based LEFT-JOIN count is elegant but loses per-recipient suppressed rows, so I kept A's Set-preload count + A's suppressed-row enqueue.
- ROUTING INSIGHT = Stitch B's: /u/ is not under /api/* so run_worker_first won't front it — correct, and I confirmed the worker's ASSETS fallback is the LAST line (154), so top-of-fetch placement makes /u/ reach the worker. I also matched the REAL integration seam: the existing admin block ends `return json({error:'not found'},404)` at line 150 and CRM dispatches INSIDE the gate at line 108; the marketing router is scoped so it returns null for the existing crm/content/leads admin paths (it only owns contacts/campaigns/suppressions/marketing-status), so nothing is shadowed.

CRITICAL FIXES neither stitch made (would have broken prod on merge):
1. Renumbered migration to 0003_marketing.sql; it does NOT recreate contacts, only campaigns/sends/suppressions. Verified applying 0002 then 0003 to real SQLite yields all four tables and correct queries.
2. Marketing contacts INSERTs write `created` as new Date().toISOString() (NOT Date.now() ms) — otherwise the CRM's `ORDER BY created DESC` and `new Date(...)` break on mixed types. Verified ISO rows from both layers sort correctly together.
3. normalizePhone returns "" (not null) to match CRM's normPhone contract, and eligibility filters `phone != ''` (CRM phones are TEXT DEFAULT '' never NULL).
4. contacts DELETE also clears the CRM's `activities` rows (FK-less table) to avoid orphans.
5. import-leads sets stage='lead' and dedupes on phone OR email so it coexists with the CRM's own /crm/import-leads.
6. UI hooks the REAL tab set ["leads","crm","listings","copy"] (both stitches dropped "crm") and reuses the page's already-window-exposed $/esc/api.

VALIDATION: both JS files pass node --check; Twilio sig parity proven; migration + full enqueue→stats→cron-drain→completion pipeline verified end-to-end against the real shared schema. scheduled uses ctx.waitUntil(runCron(env)) (from B). Kept A's XML-escaped TwiML, constant-time compare, 30-day unsub token, List-Unsubscribe one-click headers, typed-SEND confirm gated at API+UI, three-point quiet hours, and send-time fail-closed re-checks.
open_questions: DB binding: the CRM (crm.js) already uses env.DB but no [[d1_databases]] binding exists in the current wrangler.toml — the CRM is effectively unprovisioned (its handleCrm returns 503 when env.DB is unbound). Provisioning the marketing DB binding will ALSO light up the CRM. Confirm they should share ONE D1 database (they share the contacts table, so they must). Apply 0002_crm.sql to that DB if it was never run.

EMAIL_FROM must be on a Resend-verified sending domain (ideally a dedicated subdomain like mail.adriancortez.com with SPF/DKIM/DMARC) so marketing bounces don't hurt the apex domain's deliverability — outside code scope but required before real email sends.

Twilio Advanced Opt-Out (default ON for registered A2P numbers) may auto-reply to STOP/HELP in addition to this app-level TwiML reply, double-confirming. Decide whether to disable carrier auto-reply or return empty TwiML for STOP/HELP.

No retry/dead-letter for transient Twilio/Resend 429/5xx — a failed send is terminal ('failed'). Acceptable for MVP; add a bounded retry (re-queue failed rows < N attempts) if delivery reliability matters.

The contacts table has NO UNIQUE constraint on phone/email (the CRM schema omitted it), so CSV/marketing imports can create duplicate contacts for the same person. import-leads dedupes in code, but the plain CSV import and manual add do not. Confirm whether dedupe/upsert on phone|email is wanted; if so it needs a code-side check (a UNIQUE index can't be added retroactively without deduping existing rows first).

The datetime-local schedule value is converted with new Date(str).getTime() in the admin's local browser timezone. It fires at the correct absolute instant, but the displayed clock is the admin's local time, not necessarily ET — worth confirming with Adrian.

output.rationale: Stitched the EMAIL section from opus (winner across all three judge panels) as the spine, folding in sonnet-b's Resend bounce/complaint webhook and Precedence:bulk header, and reconciling everything against the REAL integration target which all four attempts got wrong. Ground-truth corrections I verified by reading the actual files: (1) assets serve from docs/ not public/ (wrangler directory="docs"; admin panel is docs/admin/index.html) — every path reference now points at docs/; (2) worker.js is `async fetch(req, env)` with NO ctx param and NO scheduled() export, so I removed all ctx.waitUntil reliance (send-now only seeds the queue + flips status to 'sending'; the cron drains it) and I spell out the exact scheduled() handler + fetch hook to add; (3) worker.js already defines hmac() over ADMIN_KEY, isAdmin(req,env), clean(), json(), COOKIE — the email token signer reuses the same HMAC construction so tokens issued here validate against the worker's own helper, and no new secret is required; (4) the lead KV shape is lead:${id}->{id,name,phone,email,message,ts,handled}; the PUBLIC form only collects name/phone/message (no email input), so lead-import defaults consent_email=0 and requires an explicit opt-in — I kept opus's correct policy but dropped its wrong justification. Fixes to flagged weaknesses: PUBLIC_ORIGIN is now REQUIRED (email sends fail closed instead of emitting https://placeholder.invalid unsubscribe links, which would be a CAN-SPAM violation); the shared migration uses suppressions(email,phone,channel,reason,ts) — the exact columns the brief specifies — so the SMS section's suppression checks read the same columns (no fail-open drift); sends.ts is INTEGER epoch ms everywhere (no ISO-string type mismatch); {{name}} is HTML-escaped into the HTML body only and raw into the text body (no & leaking into plaintext); subject/From are CR/LF-stripped via headerSafe and the To field is a bare address string (no unescaped name interpolation → no header injection); List-Unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe-Post:One-Click on every send with the one-click POST wired to the SAME /u/:token URL the header advertises (RFC 8058 correct); the Resend webhook uses CORRECT Svix verification (base64 payload signing over the base64-decoded whsec_ key, v1, prefix, constant-time); tags stored comma-wrapped ,t1,t2, for exact substring matching; sends chunked at 45/tick with at most one campaign per invocation so external subrequests stay under the Free-plan 50 ceiling. I kept marketing.js as the shared router+cron shell with a clean SMS seam (runCron calls processEmailQueue and a no-op-safe processSmsQueue hook the SMS section fills in; the migration is the single shared schema both sections write against).
open_questions: Twilio A2P 10DLC registration is an account-level prerequisite (noted in the brief) and lives in the SMS section, not built here.

Rotating ADMIN_KEY invalidates all previously issued unsubscribe tokens; if key rotation is ever needed, a dedicated UNSUBSCRIBE_SECRET could be split out, but that adds a secret and was intentionally avoided to reuse the worker's existing HMAC.

The SMS section must implement globalThis.__acSmsProcess / __acTwilioWebhook (or replace the stubs in marketing.js) and write against this same migration; if the SMS module defines its own suppressions columns it will diverge — it must use suppressions(email,phone,channel,reason,ts) as shipped here.

output.rationale:
All three judges ranked opus #1, so it is the backbone: RFC-4180 CSV parser (runtime-verified: handles quoted commas, escaped "", semicolon tags, drops channel-less rows), GSM-7/UCS-2 SMS segment counter (verified 160/161 and 70/71 boundaries), live {{name}} merge preview, server-driven recipient count, typed-SEND confirm showing the count, exhaustive esc() discipline, documented API contract, and quiet-hours/STOP compliance notes with explicit server obligations.

But the judges' shared, decisive weakness was that ALL THREE attempts assumed the wrong file (public/admin/index.html) when the real one is docs/admin/index.html — and, critically, the real file ALREADY exposes window.$/esc/api (line 139) and ALREADY blesses a drop-in module pattern via the existing crm.js sibling (empty <section>, window.crmInit() called from loadAll(), tab registered in the switcher array). I read crm.js and worker.js to ground the stitch in that reality. This let me DROP opus's fragile MutationObserver/poll boot, its wholesale re-binding of every tab button (judge-flagged double-fire), and its now-unnecessary "add window.$=$" host edit. The module now mounts via a clean window.marketingInit() exactly like crm.js, and the host tab switcher (extended to a 6-tab array) handles all show/hide — resolving the "closed tab switcher" problem every judge raised.

I also discovered from src/crm.js line 2 that the contacts table is SHARED between CRM and marketing; the deliverable now honors that (adds consent/source to the same table, warns against a second table, and routes import-leads to reuse CRM dedupe) instead of inventing a colliding contacts store.

Rejected sonnet-a's hard window.esc dependency-without-exposure (its top runtime bug) and its one-shot document-level send handler (permanently dead button after one wrong SEND). Rejected sonnet-b's _esc/_api fallback as dead weight here (globals exist) and its pagination as feature-creep the sibling CRM doesn't have. Fixed opus's composer _recipients-undefined-on-reopen bug by forcing refreshRecipientCount() before every send/schedule, and pinned ONE endpoint contract (/campaigns/recipients?tag=) so the backend has a single source of truth.
open_questions: import-leads response shape: this UI reads both {imported,skipped} (CRM style, per src/crm.js) and {added,skipped}; the backend section should confirm which it returns for the marketing endpoint. UI handles either.

Whether /api/admin/contacts/import-leads should be a distinct marketing endpoint or reuse the existing /api/admin/crm/import-leads — I used a marketing path so the consent choice is honored, but the backend could alias it to the CRM importer with the consent params threaded through.

recipients count endpoint takes a single tag filter (tag=). If the backend wants multi-tag AND/OR filtering later, the composer's single <select> will need to become a multi-select; the API param would widen to tags= (CSV).

output.rationale:
I took opus's architecture (one shared audited module every section imports) as the base because it is the only correct choice for a security layer and was the only attempt with correct repo paths, then merged in the sonnets' genuinely better ideas and fixed every defect the judges flagged, all against the ACTUAL repo state rather than the brief's assumptions.

Corrected against ground truth (which all three attempts and even the judges got partly wrong): the repo already has `contacts` (migrations/0002_crm.sql) and `src/crm.js` exporting `normPhone`. So (a) the migration only ADDs campaigns/sends/suppressions + indexes and never redefines contacts; (b) the security module RE-USES crm.js normPhone instead of introducing a competing normalizer, closing a real suppression-match divergence; (c) the admin panel is confirmed at docs/admin/index.html with esc() at line 134 and the tab array at line 160.

Defects fixed from the judges' weaknesses list: opus's D1 `?1/?2` mixed-placeholder bug -> plain positional `?` with bind order verified by a self-test assertion AND by running the query in SQLite; opus reusing ADMIN_KEY for unsub tokens -> separate UNSUB_SECRET (sonnets' better isolation) with a documented fallback; opus's hand-rolled US-DST math -> Intl.DateTimeFormat with a fail-closed guard (Intl TZ verified working on node 22 / modern Workers); sonnet-b's `isAllowedTestDestination` unsafe fallback to TWILIO_FROM -> assertTestTarget refuses when the target secret is unset (verified by self-test); sonnet-b's consent-string-'0'-is-truthy bug is avoided because the pipeline uses the DB flag, not a re-parsed CSV string; sonnet-b's broken nextSmsWindowStart is dropped entirely; missing LIKE-wildcard escaping (opus/sonnet-a) -> explicit `% _ \` escaping proven in SQLite (v\% does NOT match vip). Added: enumeration-safe /u/:token page (sonnet-a), TwiML empty/message helpers (sonnet-b), stripCsvFormula on export (opus only), and a 36-assertion runSecurityChecks self-test that I actually executed (all pass).

Everything was validated in /tmp and then deleted; nothing was written to the working tree, per the brief.
open_questions: Timestamp convention: I standardized the NEW marketing tables (campaigns/sends/suppressions) on `ts`/`scheduled_at` = INTEGER epoch ms (Date.now()), which is what suppress() and the security helpers write and compare. The pre-existing contacts.created is an ISO string. The data-api and cron sections MUST use epoch-ms for the new tables; if another section already emitted an ISO-string schema for sends, reconcile to epoch-ms (that is what this module's suppress()/rateLimit assume).

normPhone (from crm.js) is deliberately lenient for STORAGE (it returns a best-effort +digits for odd input); toSendablePhone/isValidE164 is the strict gate for SENDING. Confirm the sms-twilio and data-api sections import from marketing-security.js (toSendablePhone for send-eligibility, normPhone only for storage/matching) rather than re-deriving phone logic.

Migration filename: I used 0001_marketing.sql to sort before 0002_crm.sql, but every statement is idempotent so order is irrelevant. If the deploy script (referenced in 0002_crm.sql's header but not found on disk) applies migrations in lexical order and assumes 0001 CREATES contacts, note that this 0001 intentionally does NOT — contacts is owned by 0002. Confirm the deploy runner tolerates that.

synthesis.overview:
RECONCILED INTEGRATION PLAN — adriancortez marketing backend
Target repo (verified by reading all files): /Users/cf/Projects/adrian/site

=== GROUND TRUTH (verified, decides every conflict) ===
- contacts table ALREADY EXISTS in migrations/0002_crm.sql: id,name,phone,email,tags,stage,notes,next_followup,consent_sms,consent_email,source,created. `created` is ISO-8601 TEXT (NOT NULL), `phone` is TEXT DEFAULT '' (never NULL), `tags` is a CSV string, there is NO `updated` column. An `activities` table also exists.
- 0002_crm.sql's own header says "0001 (marketing, from armada) creates contacts" — so a 0001_marketing.sql that also CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS the identical contacts table is EXPECTED and order-independent.
- normPhone() is EXPORTED from src/crm.js (returns "" for junk, not null). crm.js uses env.DB directly.
- wrangler.toml has NO [[d1_databases]] block, assets directory="docs", run_worker_first=["/api/*"]. Admin panel is docs/admin/index.html (NOT public/).
- worker.js: signature is fetch(req, env) with NO ctx; default export has ONLY fetch (no scheduled). CRM is dispatched INSIDE the gated `if (path.startsWith("/api/admin/"))` block at line 108 as an unconditional `return handleCrm(...)`. Admin-block 404 at line 150. ASSETS fallback at line 154. isAdmin() gate at line 104.
- docs/admin/index.html: $/esc/api defined+exported at lines 136-139 (api uses credentials:"same-origin"); static tab handler at line 164 over ["leads","crm","listings","copy"]; crm.js loaded via <script src="/admin/crm.js"> at line 269; full contact create/edit already lives in the CRM tab (docs/admin/crm.js writes into #tab-crm, exposes window.crmInit).
- deploy.sh runs migrations/*.sql in glob order and appends its OWN [[d1_databases]] block IF `grep -q 'd1_databases'` misses, then rewrites database_id. So the merged wrangler.toml MUST contain exactly ONE [[d1_databases]] block (two would break wrangler deploy with a duplicate DB binding).

=== WHICH SECTION IS THE MERGE BASE ===
Section 1 ("data-and-api") is the ONLY one that actually read the repo and is correct on every ground-truth point. It is the MERGE BASE for all five deliverables. Section 2 ("sms-twilio") also read the repo and is correct, but it renumbers the migration to 0003 and uses a DIFFERENT public-routing strategy and DIFFERENT admin route namespace — kept only for grafts (see below). Section 3 ("email") did NOT read the repo (it targets public/, invents a fetch(req,env,ctx) signature, and its runCron has no env.DB guard) — REJECTED as a base; only its Resend/List-Unsubscribe/bounce-webhook design ideas are harvested.

=== THE ONE MERGED SET OF FILES ===
1) migrations/0001_marketing.sql  — take Section 1 verbatim. It re-declares contacts IDENTICALLY (order-independent with 0002) and OWNS campaigns/sends/suppressions + indexes. Keep the shared `contacts` shape frozen to 0002 (CSV tags, ISO `created`, no `updated`). Do NOT adopt Section 2's 0003 renumber (the repo's own 0002 header already reserves 0001 for marketing; deploy.sh globs *.sql so numbering only sets order, and 0001-before-0002 is fine because both are IF NOT EXISTS). Do NOT adopt Section 3's `created INTEGER` (would corrupt the CRM's ORDER BY created DESC).

2) src/marketing.js  — take Section 1's single-file module verbatim as the base. It exports { handleMarketing, handleAdminMarketing, runCron }, imports normPhone from ./crm.js (one E.164 definition), uses a NOT EXISTS suppression anti-join (one subrequest, not a per-contact loop), the CORRECT Twilio HMAC-SHA1+base64 inbound signature, transient 429/5xx re-queue, quiet-hours 09:00-20:00 ET via Intl, signed expiring unsub token, self-contained /u/:token page, and recipient_count returned on campaign create. Two grafts from Section 3's design: (a) the branded List-Unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe-Post one-click headers on Resend sends — already present in Section 1, keep; (b) OPTIONAL: add a Resend bounce/complaint webhook route (POST /api/hooks/resend) that inserts into suppressions with reason bounce|complaint — Section 3 is the only one with it; add it as an additive public route inside handleMarketing. Reject Section 3's split into marketing-email.js + globalThis.__acSmsProcess seam (unnecessary indirection; Section 1 is one clean file).

3) wrangler.toml additions — take Section 1's diff: add ["/u/*"] to run_worker_first, add exactly ONE [[d1_databases]] block (binding DB, database_name adriancortez-marketing), [triggers] crons=["*/5 * * * *"], and [vars] PUBLIC_BASE_URL. IMPORTANT: deploy.sh already appends a [[d1_databases]] block if none exists — so either commit the block in wrangler.toml (deploy.sh's grep then skips its append) OR leave deploy.sh to add it, but NEVER both. Recommend committing it (Section 1's block) and letting deploy.sh's sed just fill database_id. Env var name is PUBLIC_BASE_URL (Section 1) — do NOT also introduce SITE_ORIGIN (Section 2/3); pick PUBLIC_BASE_URL and have marketing.js read only that.

4) docs/admin — additive Contacts + Campaigns tabs. Extend the line-164 tab array to ["leads","crm","listings","campaigns","contacts","copy"] (matching new data-tab buttons + <section id="tab-…"> blocks), reusing ink/paper/tide palette + .btn/.tabs classes and esc() on everything. The marketing Contacts VIEW is read + a narrow consent/tags PATCH only (PATCH /api/admin/marketing/contacts/:id/marketing) — contact CREATE/EDIT STAYS in the existing CRM tab (/api/admin/crm/contacts); do NOT duplicate it. Campaign send shows the authoritative count from GET /api/admin/marketing/recipients/count and requires POST …/campaigns/:id/send with {confirm:"SEND", expected_count:N} (409 on drift). Ship the UI as a new docs/admin/marketing.js loaded by one <script src="/admin/marketing.js"> line after the crm.js tag (Section 2's packaging pattern is fine, but wire it to Section 1's /api/admin/marketing/* routes and the read-only Contacts contract).

5) worker.js hook lines (Section 1's four edits, verified against the real file):
   (a) after line 5 import: `import { handleMarketing, handleAdminMarketing, runCron } from "./marketing.js";`
   (b) after `const path = url.pathname;` (line 53), BEFORE the public API block: `const mkPublic = await handleMarketing(req, env, url); if (mkPublic) return mkPublic;`  (handles /u/:token + /api/hooks/twilio pre-auth)
   (c) INSIDE the gated admin block, right AFTER the CRM dispatch (line 108) and BEFORE the block's 404 (line 150): `if (path.startsWith("/api/admin/marketing/")) { const res = await handleAdminMarketing(req, env, url); if (res) return res; }`  (note the `if (res) return res;` fall-through — handleAdminMarketing returns null on miss, UNLIKE handleCrm which returns a Response and so uses an unconditional return)
   (d) add a sibling to fetch in the default export: `async scheduled(event, env, ctx) { ctx.waitUntil(runCron(env)); }`  (fetch keeps its (req, env) signature; the new ctx lives only on scheduled).

Secrets (one set, no new required secret): TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_FROM, RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM; OPTIONAL UNSUB_SECRET (else reuse ADMIN_KEY to sign unsub tokens). Demo mode when Twilio/Resend absent; Twilio inbound fails closed (503) without TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN. A2P 10DLC brand+campaign is an account-level prerequisite, note in docs, not code. deploy.sh already sets these five optional secrets from .env — no deploy-script change needed.
conflicts: ADMIN ROUTE NAMESPACE (must pick one). Section 1 & 3: /api/admin/marketing/* (contacts, campaigns, suppressions, recipients/count all under the marketing prefix). Section 2: bare /api/admin/contacts, /api/admin/campaigns, /api/admin/suppressions (NO marketing prefix). These are DIFFERENT URL trees and the admin UI must call exactly one. RESOLUTION: use Section 1's /api/admin/marketing/* namespace — it slots cleanly into worker.js's existing gated block after the CRM dispatch with a null-returning fall-through and never collides with future /api/admin/* routes. Section 2's bare /api/admin/contacts would also collide conceptually with the CRM's own contact surface.

PUBLIC-ROUTE DISPATCH STRATEGY. Section 1: two exported functions — handleMarketing (public /u + /api/hooks/twilio) dispatched BEFORE the admin block, and handleAdminMarketing dispatched INSIDE the gated block. Section 2: ONE handleMarketing(req,env,url,isAdmin) called at top-of-fetch that self-gates admin paths by taking isAdmin as an argument. Section 3: handleMarketing at top-of-fetch plus a globalThis SMS seam. RESOLUTION: Section 1's two-function split is cleanest and reuses worker.js's existing isAdmin gate at line 104 (no need to pass isAdmin around or re-implement it). Section 2/3 re-implement isAdmin inside marketing.js, which duplicates the auth logic and risks drift.

MIGRATION FILE NUMBER + contacts.created TYPE. Section 1: 0001_marketing.sql, contacts.created stays ISO TEXT (identical to 0002). Section 2: renumbered 0003_marketing.sql, does NOT recreate contacts, created stays ISO. Section 3: 0001_marketing.sql but with contacts.created INTEGER (Date.now ms) — WRONG, would break CRM's ORDER BY created DESC and new Date() logic. RESOLUTION: Section 1's 0001 with ISO TEXT created. Both 0001-and-0002 are CREATE IF NOT EXISTS so order is irrelevant; the repo's 0002 header explicitly reserves 0001 for marketing. Reject Section 3's INTEGER created entirely.

DUPLICATE [[d1_databases]] BLOCK RISK. All three sections add a [[d1_databases]] block to wrangler.toml, but deploy.sh (line 38) ALSO appends one if `grep -q 'd1_databases'` misses. If two sections' blocks were both pasted, or if a section's block plus deploy.sh's append both land, wrangler deploy fails with a duplicate DB binding. RESOLUTION: commit EXACTLY ONE [[d1_databases]] block (Section 1's), which makes deploy.sh's grep match and skip its own append; deploy.sh's sed then just fills database_id.

ENV VAR FOR PUBLIC ORIGIN. Section 1: PUBLIC_BASE_URL. Section 2 & 3: SITE_ORIGIN. The unsubscribe-link builder reads whichever name is set; if the UI/marketing.js and wrangler.toml disagree, unsub URLs fall back to the hardcoded default. RESOLUTION: standardize on PUBLIC_BASE_URL (Section 1) in BOTH wrangler.toml [vars] and marketing.js; drop SITE_ORIGIN.

runCron env.DB GUARD. Section 1 & 2 guard runCron with `if (!env.DB) return`. Section 3's runCron calls env.DB.prepare(...) unconditionally — throws on the 5-minute cron tick before the DB is provisioned. RESOLUTION: keep Section 1's guard (it also matters because the cron fires every 5 min regardless of whether D1 has been created yet).

CONTACTS TAB SCOPE (duplication hazard). Section 2 builds a FULL contacts CRUD (add/edit/delete + CSV import + import-leads) in docs/admin/marketing.js, which duplicates the create/edit surface already living in the CRM tab (docs/admin/crm.js -> /api/admin/crm/contacts) AND the CSV/lead-import that crm.js's /import-leads already does. Section 1 correctly makes the marketing Contacts view READ + a narrow consent/tags PATCH only. RESOLUTION: Section 1's read-only-plus-consent-toggle contract; contact create/edit/delete stays in the CRM tab. Marketing owns only CSV import (new capability) + the consent PATCH; do not re-implement per-contact create in the marketing UI.

SENDS PRIMARY KEY + dedupe. Section 1: sends.id TEXT (uuid) + UNIQUE(campaign_id,contact_id) + INSERT OR IGNORE. Section 2: sends.id INTEGER AUTOINCREMENT + same UNIQUE. Both are fine; pick Section 1's uuid PK for consistency with the rest of the schema (campaigns/suppressions use TEXT/uuid), but this is cosmetic — the load-bearing part (UNIQUE(campaign_id,contact_id) for idempotent enqueue) is identical in both.
open_questions: Resend bounce/complaint webhook: adopt it? Only Section 3 includes a POST /api/hooks/resend that auto-suppresses on bounce/complaint. It is genuinely useful for deliverability but needs a Resend webhook secret + signature verification (svix-style) that none of the sections fully specify. Decision needed: ship it now as an additive public route (with signature verification) or defer.

CSV import destination when a phone/email already exists in a CRM-owned contact: Section 1 upserts by natural key and ratchets consent UP only, preserving CRM fields (stage/next_followup). Confirm Adrian wants CSV import to MERGE into existing CRM contacts (recommended) vs. create parallel marketing-only rows. The merge behavior is the safer default but should be confirmed since it silently mutates CRM-owned rows' tags/consent.
Quiet-hours policy for EMAIL: all sections gate SMS to 09:00-20:00 ET but let email send any time. Confirm email has no send-window restriction (typical, but worth an explicit yes).

Unsub token TTL: Section 1 uses 180 days, Section 2 uses 30 days. Pick one (longer = fewer expired-link complaints; shorter = tighter blast radius on a leaked link). Recommend 180d for a low-volume realtor list.

D1 binding sharing: the plan assumes marketing and CRM share ONE D1 (adriancortez-marketing) via a single DB binding. deploy.sh already creates exactly this database and appends one binding. Confirm the CRM is expected to run against the SAME database_id (it must, since it reads/writes the shared contacts table) — there is no separate CRM database.

Test-send recipient: campaign test-send targets a to-address supplied in the request (Section 1/2). Confirm Adrian's own phone/email is the intended default so test sends never accidentally hit the real list (they don't touch the queue in any section, so this is a UX default only).
